name: Cajun Shepherd's Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 13x9-inch ungreased baking pan, combine the ground beef and pork. Mix in the eggs and bread crumbs by hand until thoroughly mixed. Set aside.
2. In a 1-quart saucepan, melt the butter over high heat. Add onions, celery, green bell peppers, minced garlic, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco sauce, and meat seasoning mix. Saute for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently and scraping the pan bottom well. Remove from heat and cool.
3. Add the sauteed vegetable mixture and 1/4 cup of milk to the meat mixture. Mix well by hand. Form into a 12x8-inch loaf and center in the pan.
4. Bake at 450°F until brown on top, about 30 minutes. Remove from oven; pour off drippings, reserving 2-1/2 tablespoons. Set meat and drippings aside.
5. Boil potatoes until fork tender, then drain, reserving about 1 cup of the cooking liquid. In a large mixing bowl, combine the hot potatoes with the remaining butter, milk, salt, and white pepper. Stir until broken up, then beat with a metal whisk or electric mixer with a paddle until creamy and smooth. If needed, mix in some of the reserved potato water to achieve creaminess.
6. In a large skillet (preferably non-stick), melt 2-1/2 tablespoons butter over high heat. Add carrots, onions, and vegetable seasoning mix; saute for 1-1/2 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. Add zucchini and yellow squash and continue sauteing until vegetables are noticeably brighter in color, about 3 to 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat.
8. Mound the undrained vegetables on top of the meat loaf, away from the edges. Layer the mashed potatoes evenly over the vegetables and top edges of the meat using all the potatoes. Return the pan to the oven and bake at 525°F until brown on top, about 8 to 10 minutes.
9. Serve immediately with about 1/2 cup Very Hot Cajun Sauce (see recipe) under each serving.
